metadata.description: Full decision-tree accuracy corpus for 1976 Mercedes-Benz 450SL (M117 V8, Bosch K-Jetronic CIS, 722 auto)

queries.query: The engine makes a rattling noise from the front on startup that sometimes goes away, and it seems to have lost power gradually.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the timing chain, timing chain guides (upper left rail, upper right rail, and lower tensioning rail), and the chain tensioner. The M117 uses a duplex (double-row) timing chain. The plastic/nylon timing chain guide rails become brittle with age and heat, eventually cracking and breaking. Broken guide pieces get dragged into the chain/sprocket area and can cause the chain to jump, which bends valves (pistons contact valves). Replacement requires removing the valve covers, radiator, fan, and timing cover. Set engine to TDC before disassembly. Use genuine Mercedes or Febi/Iwis replacement parts. Chain tensioner: replace with a new spring-loaded tensioner (Mercedes A1170500211 or equivalent). NOTE: This is the #1 catastrophic failure on the M117. If the chain has never been replaced and the car has 60,000+ miles, this service is urgent and should not be delayed. A rattling noise on startup is an early warning — driving with a rattling chain risks total engine destruction. Chain replacement interval is approximately 100,000 miles, but guide condition should be inspected at 60,000.

queries.query: The car puffs blue smoke from the exhaust for a few seconds every time I start it in the morning, then it clears up.
queries.validated_fix: Replace all 16 valve stem seals. The original seals harden and shrink with age, allowing oil to seep down the valve stems into the combustion chambers while the engine sits. Remove the camshafts and rocker assemblies, use compressed air through the spark plug holes to hold the valves up, and replace the seals with modern Viton-type seals (Elring or Victor Reinz). NOTE: If the engine also smokes under sustained acceleration (not just startup), the piston rings are likely worn as well — a compression test will differentiate. Valve seal replacement alone is a top-end job that does not require engine removal.

queries.query: There's oil seeping down both sides of the engine and I can smell burning oil after driving, especially in traffic.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the valve cover gaskets with new rubber gaskets (Elring or Victor Reinz). Clean both the cover rails and the head surfaces thoroughly. The M117 aluminum valve covers can warp — check flatness with a straightedge and carefully flatten on a surface plate if warped. Torque valve cover bolts to 8-10 Nm in an alternating pattern. Do NOT over-tighten — this warps the covers and guarantees future leaks. NOTE: Also replace the spark plug tube seals (located at each plug hole in the valve cover) at the same time — these are a separate seal from the main gasket and commonly leak oil into the spark plug wells.

queries.query: There's a constant oil drip from the back of the engine near the transmission bellhousing — it leaves a big puddle.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the rear crankshaft main seal. The transmission must be removed to access it. Clean the crankshaft seal surface. Install a new seal (Corteco/Elring) with the sealing lip facing the engine. Apply a thin bead of Hylomar or Loctite 518 at the seal-to-block junction. NOTE: If the crank has a groove worn into the seal contact area, a Speedi-Sleeve can provide a fresh sealing surface. While the transmission is out, also replace the pilot bearing and inspect the flexplate for cracks (a common hidden issue on the M117).

queries.query: There's a persistent oil drip from the bottom center of the engine that leaves spots wherever I park.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the oil pan gasket. Drain the oil, remove the oil pan (requires loosening/supporting the engine slightly on the R107). Clean both mating surfaces thoroughly. Install a new gasket (Elring or Victor Reinz) — the M117 uses a multi-piece cork/rubber gasket. Apply RTV sealant at the timing cover junction corners. Torque pan bolts to 10 Nm — do not over-tighten cork gaskets. NOTE: While the pan is off, inspect the oil pump pickup screen for debris and the oil pump for wear. Clean any sludge from the pan and the oil pickup.

queries.query: There's oil seeping from the front of the engine, around and behind the crankshaft pulley area.
queries.validated_fix: Remove the harmonic balancer, fan, and timing chain cover. Replace the cover gasket and the front crankshaft seal. Clean all surfaces. NOTE: Since this is a major front-of-engine job, it is highly advisable to replace the timing chain, guides, and tensioner at the same time (see Timing Chain entry). The labor overlap makes this the ideal time to address both issues in one teardown.

queries.query: The temperature gauge climbs toward the red zone in traffic or on hot days — the car never used to run this hot.
queries.validated_fix: Systematic diagnosis: (1) Replace the thermostat — the M117 uses an 80°C (176°F) or 87°C (189°F) thermostat. A stuck-closed thermostat is the most common cause. (2) Have the radiator rodded/cleaned or replace it — the original copper/brass radiator clogs with sediment over decades. Upgrade to an aluminum radiator for superior cooling. (3) Replace the water pump if the bearing is noisy or the weep hole drips (Meyle or Graf). (4) Check the fan clutch — it should lock up when hot and pull significant air. Replace if it spins freely when engine is hot (Sachs/Behr). (5) Flush the cooling system with new 50/50 coolant — use Mercedes-approved blue coolant. NOTE: The 1976 US-spec 450SL has the catalytic converters mounted directly on the exhaust manifolds ('warm-up catalysts'), which generate tremendous underhood heat. Mercedes relocated them in later years. This underhood heat contributes to numerous heat-related issues including overheating, vapor lock, and premature component degradation.

queries.query: There's a noticeable vibration in the cabin, especially at idle, and a thud when I shift from Park to Drive.
queries.validated_fix: Replace both engine mounts. The M117 mounts are hydraulic (fluid-filled) rubber mounts that collapse and tear over time. Use Meyle or Lemforder replacement mounts (Mercedes A1072412313 right, A1072412413 left). Support the engine with a jack under the oil pan (with a block of wood), remove the old mounts, and install new ones. Torque to spec. NOTE: Failed engine mounts cause the engine to drop, which can stress the transmission mount, crack exhaust connections, and alter throttle linkage geometry. Also replace the transmission mount at the same time if it shows any deterioration.

queries.query: The engine idles way too fast — around 1,200-1,500 RPM instead of the normal 700-800 RPM, and it won't come down.
queries.technical_issue: Idle Speed Too High / Auxiliary Air Valve Stuck Open
queries.validated_fix: Check and replace the auxiliary air valve (AAV, also called the auxiliary air slide). This thermostatically-controlled valve allows extra air into the intake during warm-up and should close fully when the engine reaches operating temperature. If the bimetallic element fails or the valve sticks open, the idle stays high. Located on the intake manifold, it can be tested by disconnecting the air hose — if idle drops when blocked, the valve is stuck open. Replace with a new unit (Bosch 0280140010 or Mercedes equivalent). Also check for vacuum leaks — any unmetered air entering the intake after the air sensor plate raises the idle. NOTE: The idle speed adjustment screw is on the throttle body. Do not adjust it to compensate for a stuck AAV — fix the root cause.

queries.query: The oil looks milky and the car overheats quickly — I see bubbles in the coolant when the radiator cap is off.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the head gaskets (both sides on the V8). Remove the intake manifold, exhaust manifolds, and both cylinder heads. Inspect the head and block deck surfaces for warpage with a precision straightedge (max .05mm over any 150mm span). Resurface if needed. Use new Elring or Victor Reinz head gasket sets. Torque head bolts in the factory sequence to the specified values (M117 uses stretch bolts that should be replaced, not reused). Flush the cooling system and change the oil immediately after first startup and again at 500 miles. NOTE: Determine root cause — detonation, overheating, or incorrect torque on a previous assembly. A chemical combustion gas test (block tester) can confirm a head gasket leak before disassembly.

queries.query: The car starts fine cold but dies or runs terribly after about 2 minutes of warming up — then won't restart until it cools down.
queries.validated_fix: Replace or rebuild the warm-up regulator (WUR). The WUR controls the CIS system's control pressure, which determines the fuel mixture enrichment during warm-up. An internal bimetallic strip and electric heating element gradually increase control pressure as the engine warms. If the heating element fails, the regulator stays in 'cold enrichment' mode, flooding the engine when warm. If it fails the opposite way, the engine runs too lean when cold. Test: measure control pressure with a CIS fuel pressure gauge — cold should be approximately 1.5-2.0 bar, rising to 3.2-3.8 bar when warm. Replace with a Bosch or Mercedes unit. NOTE: The WUR electrical connector and vacuum line must both be connected for proper operation. A disconnected vacuum line or corroded electrical connector can cause identical symptoms.

queries.query: The engine runs unevenly — some cylinders seem to fire while others miss. It surges and stumbles at all RPMs.
queries.technical_issue: Fuel Distributor — Sticking or Worn Plunger
queries.validated_fix: Inspect and rebuild or replace the CIS fuel distributor (Bosch 0438100082 or equivalent for the M117). The fuel distributor is the heart of the K-Jetronic system — it meters fuel to each cylinder via individual injector lines based on the position of the air sensor plate. Internal corrosion or varnish causes the metering plunger to stick or wear unevenly, resulting in inconsistent fuel delivery. A rebuild involves disassembly, cleaning, and replacement of internal seals and the metering plunger. NOTE: Never disassemble the fuel distributor unless you have the proper CIS pressure gauge set and experience — incorrect reassembly will make the car undriveable. Genuine Bosch rebuilds or rebuilt units from reputable Mercedes CIS specialists are recommended. Also verify fuel filter is fresh and fuel pressure is within spec before blaming the distributor.

queries.query: The engine runs rough at idle and I can smell raw gasoline from the engine bay. Some plugs are fouled black.
queries.technical_issue: Fuel Injector Leak / Poor Spray Pattern
queries.validated_fix: Test and replace the CIS injectors. The 1976 450SL uses Bosch mechanical (continuous injection) injectors that open at a specific pressure (~3.3 bar). Over time the pintle seats wear and the injectors leak down, dripping fuel into the ports when the engine is off (causing hard hot starts and raw fuel smell). Test: remove injectors and observe spray pattern while cranking — they should produce a fine, conical mist. Replace leaking injectors with new Bosch injectors. The 1976 uses steel-body injectors; later brass versions are more corrosion-resistant and are a recommended upgrade. NOTE: Always replace all 8 injectors as a set for even fuel distribution.

queries.query: The car starts perfectly cold but after driving and shutting it off for 10-20 minutes, it refuses to start or takes very long cranking.
queries.technical_issue: Hard Hot Start — Fuel System Heat Soak
queries.validated_fix: Multiple factors contribute — address all: (1) Replace leaking CIS fuel injectors (they drip fuel into hot ports after shutdown, flooding the cylinders). (2) Check/replace the fuel accumulator — it maintains residual fuel pressure after shutdown; a failed accumulator lets pressure bleed off, causing vapor in the lines. (3) Replace the check valve in the fuel pump or the fuel pump itself if it doesn't hold residual pressure. (4) Install heat shielding on the fuel lines near the exhaust manifolds and the manifold-mounted catalytic converters (1976 specific). (5) Replace the warm-up regulator if control pressure is incorrect. NOTE: The 1976 450SL's underhood catalytic converters generate extreme heat — this is the single biggest contributor to hot-start problems. Many owners install thermal wrap on the catalysts and fuel lines, or relocate the cats further downstream.

queries.query: The car cranks but won't start — there's no fuel getting to the engine. I can't hear the fuel pump running.
queries.validated_fix: Check/replace the fuel pump relay. Located above the fuse box (under the hood on the passenger side of the firewall on the 1976), the relay energizes the electric fuel pump. Test by swapping with an identical relay from the same relay panel. Also check the fuel pump safety switch (on the air sensor plate) — if the sensor plate is stuck or the connector is corroded, the safety circuit keeps the pump off. To bypass for testing: unplug the blue safety switch connector — the pump should run with ignition on. If the pump now runs, the safety switch needs cleaning or replacement. NOTE: The 1976 450SL uses a Bosch electric fuel pump in the rear of the car. If the relay and safety switch are good but the pump doesn't run, the pump itself has failed (Bosch 0580254957 or equivalent).

queries.query: After the car sits for just a short time, it takes a lot of cranking to start — like the fuel system loses pressure when I turn it off.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the fuel accumulator. The accumulator maintains residual fuel pressure in the CIS system after engine shutdown, preventing vapor formation and ensuring quick restarts. A failed accumulator lets pressure bleed down. Located near the fuel pump under the car, replace with a Bosch unit. Also check the fuel pump check valve — if it allows fuel to drain back to the tank, the accumulator alone won't fix the problem. NOTE: A bad accumulator also contributes to hot-start problems because the fuel vaporizes more easily without maintained pressure.

queries.query: The car is very hard to start on cold mornings — it cranks and cranks but barely catches unless I hold the throttle open.
queries.technical_issue: Cold Start Valve / Thermo-Time Switch Failure
queries.validated_fix: Check the cold-start injector (5th injector) and the thermo-time switch. The cold-start injector provides extra fuel enrichment during cranking in cold conditions. The thermo-time switch (mounted in the cylinder head coolant passage) controls how long the cold-start injector operates. Test the cold-start injector by checking for fuel delivery during cranking with the engine cold. Test the thermo-time switch with an ohmmeter — it should have continuity below approximately 35°C and open circuit above. Replace whichever component has failed (Bosch cold-start injector and thermo-time switch). NOTE: If both are functioning and cold starts are still poor, check the warm-up regulator and control pressure — insufficient enrichment from the WUR also causes poor cold starting.

queries.query: The engine has a terrible flat spot on acceleration — a big hesitation when I push the throttle from idle.
queries.validated_fix: Inspect and adjust the CIS air sensor plate. The plate must move freely in the air cone without friction or binding. Check for contamination (dirt, debris) in the air cone. Verify the sensor plate is centered — it should not touch the sides of the cone. Check that the plate's rest position is correct (flush with the bottom of the cone or slightly below per the factory specification). Lubricate the pivot with CIS-approved lubricant. Also check the fuel distributor plunger for binding (connected to the sensor plate via a lever). NOTE: The air sensor plate position directly controls fuel metering. Any binding, misalignment, or contamination in this area causes driveability issues throughout the RPM range. Do NOT bend or force the plate — it must be adjusted with the proper gauges.

queries.query: The car lacks power and seems to starve for fuel at higher RPMs — it runs okay at idle but bogs down under load.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the fuel filter. The Bosch CIS system is very sensitive to fuel contamination — even minor restrictions in fuel flow cause driveability problems under load. The filter is located under the car near the fuel pump. Replace with a genuine Bosch or Mann filter. Also inspect the fuel tank for rust and sediment — on a 50-year-old car, internal tank corrosion is common. If the tank is contaminated, it should be professionally cleaned and sealed (POR-15 tank sealant) or replaced. NOTE: The CIS fuel filter should be replaced every 15,000 miles or annually as preventive maintenance.

queries.query: The engine misfires and runs rough, especially in humid or wet weather — it stumbles and barely runs when damp.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the distributor cap and rotor. The M117 uses a large V8 distributor cap that is prone to carbon tracking and hairline cracks that allow moisture to short-circuit the spark. Use a quality Bosch or Beru replacement cap and rotor. Also replace the spark plug wires (Beru or Bosch silicone 7mm) — cracked wire insulation bleeds spark energy to ground, especially when wet. NOTE: The 1976 450SL uses a transistorized ignition system (no conventional points) — but the cap, rotor, and plug wires are still standard maintenance items that wear out.

queries.query: The engine has a persistent misfire at higher RPMs and I can sometimes see sparking at the plug wires in the dark.
queries.validated_fix: Replace all 8 spark plug wires plus the coil wire with a matched set (Beru or Bosch). Use proper boot grease on the distributor cap and spark plug connections. Route the wires per factory layout, keeping them separated from each other and from heat sources. NOTE: The M117 plug wires are long (V8 configuration) and expensive — but cheap generic wires cause more problems than they solve. Use OE-quality German-made wires.

queries.query: The engine pings under load and feels sluggish — it also seems to run hotter than normal.
queries.technical_issue: Ignition Timing Incorrect / Distributor Advance Issues
queries.validated_fix: Set the ignition timing with a timing light. The 1976 M117 specification is approximately 5° BTDC at idle (verify with your specific model's sticker under the hood or the factory service manual). Disconnect the vacuum advance hose and plug it before setting base timing. Check the vacuum advance canister for proper operation (apply vacuum with a hand pump — the plate should advance smoothly and hold). Check the centrifugal advance weights under the distributor rotor — they can stick from dried lubricant. NOTE: The 1976 US-spec car has emission-related timing that is more retarded than European specs. Running with timing too advanced on low-octane fuel causes detonation; too retarded causes poor performance and overheating. Use 91+ octane premium fuel.

queries.query: The car suddenly died and won't restart — there's no spark at all from the coil.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the transistorized ignition control unit (EZL module or ignition amplifier). Located on the driver's side inner fender or near the coil (varies by exact model year within the 1976 production). The transistor module replaces conventional ignition points and is the primary switching device for the ignition coil. When it fails, you lose all spark. Also check the ignition trigger sensor inside the distributor (a magnetic pickup that signals the module) — check for proper air gap and resistance. Replace the ignition coil if also suspect. NOTE: Heat is the #1 killer of transistorized ignition modules on the M117. Relocating the module to a cooler location or adding a heat sink can extend its life.

queries.query: The engine runs rough, misfires occasionally, and fuel economy has gotten worse.
queries.technical_issue: Spark Plugs — Incorrect Type or Worn
queries.validated_fix: Replace the spark plugs with the correct type: Bosch W7DC (or equivalent NGK BP6ES) for the 1976 M117. Gap to 0.8mm (0.032"). The M117 is sensitive to plug type — do not use platinum or iridium plugs in the CIS system; they change the flame kernel in ways that can affect CIS mixture calibration. Standard copper-core plugs are correct. Inspect the old plugs for diagnostic clues: tan = correct mixture; black/sooty = rich (check CIS); white/blistered = lean; oily = valve seals or rings. NOTE: Replace every 15,000-20,000 miles.

queries.query: The engine overheats rapidly (stuck closed) or takes forever to warm up and the heater barely works (stuck open).
queries.validated_fix: Replace the thermostat. The M117 uses an 80°C or 87°C thermostat located in the thermostat housing at the front of the engine. Replace with a Wahler or Behr unit. Also replace the thermostat housing gasket/O-ring. Test the old thermostat in boiling water to confirm failure mode. NOTE: Running without a thermostat (removed to 'fix' overheating) actually worsens the problem at highway speed because coolant flows too fast through the radiator and doesn't have time to shed heat.

queries.query: I see coolant dripping from behind the fan area and the engine has started running hot.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the water pump (Graf, Meyle, or Mercedes OE). The M117 water pump is driven by the serpentine belt. Replace the gasket/O-ring and refill/bleed the cooling system after installation. NOTE: When replacing the water pump, also replace the fan clutch (Sachs/Behr) — they have similar service life and the fan must be removed to access the pump anyway.

queries.query: The engine overheats in traffic and at low speed but runs cool on the highway — the fan doesn't seem to pull much air.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the viscous fan clutch (Sachs/Behr). When cold, the clutch should allow some slip; when hot, it should engage and the fan should roar. Test: a hot engine should have a very stiff fan that's difficult to spin by hand and makes noticeable air noise. If it spins freely when hot, the silicone coupling has failed. Replace as a unit. NOTE: Also ensure the fan shroud is intact and properly installed — without the shroud, even a good fan clutch can't pull enough air through the radiator at idle.

queries.query: The passenger footwell is wet and the windshield fogs with a sweet-smelling oily film — I think antifreeze is leaking inside the car.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the heater core. On the R107, the heater core is located inside the heater box behind the dashboard on the passenger side. Access requires removing the glove box, lower dash panels, and the heater box cover. Replace with a new Behr or Nissens core. Replace all hoses and clamps while the system is open. Flush the cooling system after installation. NOTE: This is a labor-intensive job due to the dashboard disassembly required. The heater control valve (vacuum-operated, on the firewall) should also be replaced at the same time — a leaking control valve is sometimes mistaken for a heater core leak.

queries.query: I keep losing coolant but can't find a leak — the overflow tank has a hairline crack that only weeps when hot and pressurized.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the coolant expansion tank. The plastic tank becomes brittle with age and heat cycling, developing hairline cracks that only leak under pressure. Replace with a new tank and a new pressure cap (1.4 bar). Inspect all radiator hoses at the same time and replace any that are swollen, soft, or cracked. NOTE: On the R107, also check the radiator neck and transmission cooler fittings in the radiator for weeping — these are other subtle leak points.

queries.query: There's a puddle of red fluid under the center of the car every time I park — the transmission is leaking badly.
queries.technical_issue: Transmission Fluid Leak — Front Pump Seal or Pan Gasket
queries.validated_fix: Identify the leak source: (1) Pan gasket: drop the pan, clean surfaces, install new gasket (cork or reusable rubber), torque to 10 Nm. (2) Front pump seal: requires transmission removal; replace the seal and inspect the torque converter hub. (3) Rear extension seal: replace at the output shaft/driveshaft connection. (4) Modulator valve: if ATF is found in the vacuum line to the modulator, replace the modulator. Use Dexron III or Mercedes-approved ATF. NOTE: The 722 transmission is extremely reliable when properly maintained — fluid and filter changes every 30,000 miles are essential. Neglected fluid causes premature wear of the clutch packs and bands.

queries.query: The transmission shifts hard — it bangs into each gear instead of shifting smoothly. Sometimes it hesitates before shifting.
queries.validated_fix: Multiple causes: (1) Check the vacuum modulator on the transmission — it controls shift quality based on engine load (vacuum). If the diaphragm is ruptured, shifts become harsh. Replace if ATF is found in the vacuum line. (2) Adjust or replace the throttle/kickdown linkage — the Bowden cable from the throttle to the transmission must be properly adjusted for correct shift timing and firmness. (3) Perform a fluid and filter change — old, burned fluid causes harsh shifts. (4) If the above don't resolve it, the valve body may need cleaning or rebuilding — sticky shift valves cause delayed or harsh engagement. NOTE: The 722.3 (in the 1976 450SL) is a 3-speed automatic. Proper Bowden cable adjustment is critical — too tight causes late, harsh shifts; too loose causes early, soft shifts.

queries.query: The transmission stays in second gear and won't shift up — the engine revs really high on the highway.
queries.technical_issue: Transmission Won't Shift into Higher Gears
queries.validated_fix: Check the governor on the output shaft — a stuck governor prevents upshifts. Also check the vacuum modulator and its vacuum supply hose — a disconnected or leaking hose can prevent shifts. Inspect the shift linkage from the column/console to the transmission — incorrect adjustment can mechanically prevent the selector valve from reaching the correct positions. If mechanical checks are clear, the valve body requires removal and inspection — stuck shift valves or debris in the valve body passages prevent proper hydraulic operation. NOTE: Many R107 owners who encounter this issue discover that a simple vacuum hose to the modulator had cracked or fallen off — always check the simplest things first.

queries.query: There's a clunk when shifting into Drive or Reverse and a vibration under the car at idle.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the transmission crossmember mount. The rubber mount between the transmission tail and the crossmember collapses over time. Replace with a Lemforder or Meyle mount. Inspect the crossmember for rust/cracking while it's accessible. NOTE: A failed trans mount combined with failed engine mounts allows excessive drivetrain movement, stresses the driveshaft, and can cause the shift linkage to bind. Replace all mounts as a set.

queries.query: I have random electrical problems — lights flicker, gauges act up, and things work intermittently with no pattern.
queries.validated_fix: Systematically clean and restore all major electrical connectors. The R107's wiring connectors use tin-plated terminals that corrode over time. Pull apart each connector, clean the terminals with electrical contact cleaner and fine sandpaper, apply dielectric grease, and reassemble. Pay special attention to: fuse box connections, headlight connectors, engine harness-to-body harness junction, alternator connections, and all ground points. Sand ground lugs to bare metal and apply dielectric grease. NOTE: If the wiring insulation is brittle and crumbling, consider a complete wiring harness replacement. Mercedes reproduction harnesses are available from specialists. Bad grounds cause more electrical problems than any other single issue.

queries.query: The battery keeps dying and the charge warning light stays on while driving.
queries.validated_fix: Test the charging system: with the engine running at 2,000 RPM, battery terminals should read 13.8-14.4V. If below 13V, the alternator is not charging. Check the belt tension and condition first (Bosch alternator on the M117). Check the voltage regulator (internal to the alternator on later models, external on some 1976 units). Replace the alternator if the brushes are worn or the rectifier has failed. If overcharging (over 15V), replace the voltage regulator. NOTE: Also check the fusible link and all wiring between the alternator and battery — corrosion in the charging circuit causes resistance that mimics alternator failure.

queries.query: My speedometer, tachometer, or fuel gauge has stopped working or reads inaccurately.
queries.validated_fix: The R107 instrument cluster uses a printed circuit board behind the gauge faces. The circuit board traces develop cracks and corrosion over time, causing gauge failures. Remove the instrument cluster and inspect the circuit board for broken traces, corrosion, and burnt connections. Repair traces with conductive paint or solder. Clean all connector pins. Also check individual sending units (fuel level sender in the tank, temperature sender on the engine). The speedometer is cable-driven — a broken cable causes a dead speedometer (replace the cable). NOTE: Rebuilt/restored R107 instrument clusters are available from Mercedes instrument specialists. The voltage regulator inside the cluster (for the fuel and temp gauges) can also fail — test and replace if needed.

queries.query: My headlights flicker or go out while driving — sometimes jiggling the switch brings them back.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the headlight switch and inspect/replace the headlight relay. The R107's headlight circuit passes through the column-mounted switch, which handles high current and burns the contacts over time. Replace the switch (Mercedes A1075450010 or equivalent reproduction). Install a headlight relay upgrade kit to route the high current through relays rather than through the switch — this is the permanent fix. NOTE: Check and clean all headlight ground connections — corroded grounds cause dim or flickering headlights independent of the switch condition.

queries.query: The door locks don't all lock/unlock together anymore — some doors lock and others don't, or nothing happens at all.
queries.validated_fix: The R107 uses a vacuum-operated central locking system (not electric). The vacuum is supplied by the engine (gas models) or a separate vacuum pump (diesel). Diagnose: check the vacuum supply to the lock system — a cracked or disconnected rubber hose causes total failure. Check individual door lock actuators — the rubber diaphragms inside the actuators crack and lose vacuum (replace the actuator). Check the vacuum reservoir (a small canister that stores vacuum for when the engine is off). Check the vacuum switch in the driver's door lock mechanism. Replace all cracked vacuum lines with new rubber hose. NOTE: A hissing sound when operating locks indicates a vacuum leak — follow the sound to locate the cracked hose or failed actuator diaphragm.

queries.query: The wipers either don't work at all, only work on one speed, or won't park properly in the down position.
queries.validated_fix: Check the wiper relay (located in the relay panel above the fuse box). Also check the wiper switch on the steering column. Test the wiper motor by applying 12V directly — if it runs, the fault is in the relay, switch, or wiring. If it doesn't run or runs slowly, replace the wiper motor. On the R107, the wiper linkage can also bind — lubricate the pivot points and linkage. Replace the wiper blades while servicing. NOTE: The wiper park mechanism is a separate contact inside the motor — if the wipers run but don't park, the motor's park switch has failed.

queries.query: The car barely cranks when the engine is hot — the starter labors and sometimes just clicks after a hot drive.
queries.technical_issue: Starter Motor — Slow Cranking or Heat Soak
queries.validated_fix: The starter motor is heat-soaked from the catalytic converters and exhaust manifolds. Replace the starter with a new or rebuilt unit (Bosch). Install a starter heat shield to protect it from radiant exhaust heat. Clean and tighten all battery cable connections. Check the battery — a marginal battery that works cold may fail under the additional load of a heat-soaked starter. NOTE: The 1976 US-spec 450SL with manifold-mounted catalysts generates tremendous heat near the starter. Many owners install a thermal blanket around the starter or ceramic-coat the nearby exhaust components.

queries.query: I've been told the front subframe has a crack in it and the car is unsafe to drive.
queries.validated_fix: CRITICAL SAFETY ISSUE: The 450SL front subframe is subject to Mercedes Service Campaign SIL 33/001 (issued 11/1/1985) for cracking at the lower control arm mounting tabs. This is still honored by Mercedes-Benz dealers — contact your dealer with your VIN to check if your car has been repaired. If not, the dealer will either weld reinforcing gussets to the subframe (if uncracked) or replace the entire subframe with a redesigned unit (if cracked). The replacement subframe has boxed/reinforced control arm attachment tabs. NOTE: This is a safety-critical recall that affects ALL 350/450 SL/SLC models. A cracked subframe can cause the lower control arm to separate from the car, resulting in total loss of steering control. If your car has never had this service, DO NOT DRIVE IT until it is inspected. The repair is free from Mercedes-Benz.

queries.query: The front end clunks over bumps and the steering feels vague — the car wanders at highway speed.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the front upper and lower control arm bushings (Lemforder or Meyle). The R107 uses rubber bushings that deteriorate over time. Press out the old bushings and press in new ones (or replace the entire control arm if the housing is damaged). Also replace the lower ball joints (Lemforder 1153331127) and upper ball joints (Meyle 1153330827) at the same time — they have similar service life. A front-end alignment is mandatory after any suspension component replacement. NOTE: The R107 front suspension is expensive to fully rebuild but the improvement in handling and safety is dramatic. Consider replacing all bushings, ball joints, idler arm, tie rod ends, center drag link, and shock absorbers as a comprehensive front-end restoration.

queries.query: The steering has a lot of play in the center and the power steering fluid level keeps dropping — I can see it leaking from the steering box.
queries.validated_fix: The Mercedes recirculating-ball steering box can be rebuilt or replaced. For minor play: adjust the sector shaft adjustment screw (on top of the box) to reduce center deadband — turn clockwise 1/8 turn at a time until play is minimized without binding. For leaks: rebuild the box with new seals (rebuild kits available from Febi), or replace with a remanufactured unit. Also replace the flexible steering coupling (Guibo/flex disc) on the steering shaft — if it's deteriorated, it adds play. NOTE: Also replace the steering damper (Stabilus 0004635132) — a worn damper allows shimmy and vibration. The three steering box mounting bolts should be checked — the chassis can crack at the steering box mount on heavily corroded cars.

queries.query: The steering feels imprecise and the front tires are wearing unevenly — the inner edges are bald.
queries.technical_issue: Worn Tie Rod Ends / Idler Arm
queries.validated_fix: Replace the tie rod assemblies (Febi 1073300103 left and right) and the idler arm (Febi 1264600819). Check the center drag link (Meyle 1074600605) for play — replace if worn. Inspect the pitman arm on the steering box output. Torque all castle nuts to spec and install new cotter pins. A professional front-end alignment is mandatory after replacement. NOTE: If the steering wheel shakes at speed (shimmy), the steering damper is also likely worn — replace it (Stabilus or Bilstein).

queries.query: The car wallows and bounces over bumps — the ride has gotten floaty and the body rolls excessively in corners.
queries.technical_issue: Worn Shock Absorbers — Front and Rear
queries.validated_fix: Replace all four shock absorbers. Front: KYB KG4522 or Bilstein B4/B6 for the R107. Rear: KYB KG5517 or Bilstein equivalents. The R107 uses conventional tube shocks front and rear. Inspect the shock mounts and rubber bushings — replace if deteriorated. NOTE: If you want improved handling, Bilstein Sport or Koni adjustable shocks are excellent upgrades that maintain the ride quality character of the R107 while significantly reducing body roll and improving damping.

queries.query: There's a clunk from the rear when accelerating from a stop and a vibration at highway speed.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the rear differential subframe mounts and the differential nose mount. The R107 rear suspension uses a fully independent rear end with the differential mounted in a subframe. The rubber mounts deteriorate and allow the differential to move. Replace all mounts (Lemforder or Meyle). Also check and replace the rear trailing arm bushings and the rear sway bar bushings. NOTE: Worn rear mounts also cause the driveshaft to operate at incorrect angles, which causes vibration and premature U-joint wear.

queries.query: The car pulls to one side when braking and one front wheel is much hotter than the other after driving.
queries.validated_fix: Rebuild or replace the sticking brake caliper(s). The R107 uses single-piston sliding calipers on the front. The caliper slides corrode and bind, causing uneven pad wear and pull. Remove the caliper, clean the slide pins thoroughly, replace the slide pin boots, and lubricate with silicone brake grease. If the piston is stuck or the bore is corroded, rebuild the caliper with a new piston seal kit or replace the caliper entirely. Replace the brake pads on both sides as a set. NOTE: Also replace the rubber brake hoses (Ate or ATE-branded) — old hoses can collapse internally and act as a check valve, trapping pressure at one caliper.

queries.query: The brake pedal slowly sinks to the floor when I hold it at a stop light — but there's no visible external leak.
queries.technical_issue: Brake Fluid Contamination / Master Cylinder Failure
queries.validated_fix: Replace the brake master cylinder (Ate or FTE). Bench-bleed the new master cylinder before installation. Bleed the entire brake system (starting at the furthest wheel from the master cylinder). Use DOT 4 brake fluid (Mercedes specification). If the fluid is dark or contaminated, flush the entire system. Also inspect and replace all rubber brake hoses if they're original — internal deterioration causes spongy pedal feel. NOTE: On the R107 with power brakes, also check the vacuum brake booster — a failing booster causes hard pedal and poor braking assist. Test by pressing the brake pedal with the engine off (pump several times to deplete vacuum), then start the engine — the pedal should drop slightly as vacuum assist engages. If it doesn't, the booster is failing.

queries.query: The rear brakes aren't contributing much stopping power and I hear scraping/grinding from the rear wheels.
queries.technical_issue: Rear Brake Drum/Disc Issues (Varies by Spec)
queries.validated_fix: The 1976 450SL may have rear drum brakes or rear disc brakes depending on specification. For drums: replace the brake shoes, wheel cylinders, and hardware. Check the drums for scoring and resurface or replace as needed. Adjust the shoes to proper clearance. For discs: replace the pads, check the rotors for minimum thickness and scoring, and rebuild/replace the rear calipers as needed. In both cases, check and adjust the parking brake mechanism. NOTE: Many R107 owners upgrade rear drums to rear disc brakes using later R107 components. Also check the rear brake proportioning valve — a stuck valve can starve the rear brakes of hydraulic pressure.

queries.query: The steering wheel and brake pedal pulsate when I press the brakes — it's like a rhythmic shudder.
queries.technical_issue: Brake Rotor Warping / Vibration Under Braking
queries.validated_fix: Replace or resurface the front brake rotors. Check rotor thickness and lateral runout with a dial indicator (max .05mm runout). If warped beyond spec, replace rather than machine — rotors that are close to minimum thickness after machining will warp again quickly. Use quality rotors (Zimmermann, ATE, or Brembo) and new brake pads (Textar or Jurid). Bed the brakes in properly: 10 moderate stops from 60 km/h, then 3 hard stops from 100 km/h, then allow to cool. NOTE: Rotor warping is often caused by uneven lug nut torque — always torque wheel bolts to 110 Nm in a star pattern.

queries.query: The body panels below the doors are rusting through — the metal is soft and flaking away.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the rusted rocker panels/sills. The R107's sills are structural and support the body rigidity, especially important on a roadster. Reproduction sill panels are available. Cut out the rusted sections, fit new panels, and MIG weld in place. Seal all seams with automotive seam sealer. Cavity-wax the interior of the sill after repair. Prime and paint. NOTE: Pre-1976 R107s had poorer corrosion protection from the factory. Sill rust is a structural concern — severely rusted sills compromise body rigidity and safety. Professional repair by a body shop experienced with R107s is recommended.

queries.query: The rear fenders behind the wheels are rusting from the inside out — the paint is bubbling badly.
queries.technical_issue: Rear Wheel Arch / Quarter Panel Rust
queries.validated_fix: Repair or replace the rusted wheel arch sections. Reproduction repair panels are available from R107-specialist suppliers. Cut out the corroded area, fit the new panel, and MIG weld. Treat adjacent areas with rust encapsulator. Seal, prime, and paint. Apply cavity wax inside the panel after repair. NOTE: Wheel arch rust is often caused by moisture trapped between the inner and outer panels, or by clogged drain holes in the fender wells. After repair, ensure all drainage paths are clear and apply corrosion-preventive treatments to hidden areas.

queries.query: The trunk floor is rusted through and I found standing water in the spare tire well.
queries.technical_issue: Trunk Floor / Spare Tire Well Rust
queries.validated_fix: Replace the trunk floor panel. Cut out the rusted metal, fit a new panel (fabricated or reproduction), and MIG weld. Seal all seams and coat with epoxy primer and undercoating. Also reseal the taillamp gaskets and trunk lid weatherstrip — these are the primary water entry points. Check and clear the trunk drain holes. NOTE: Trunk floor rust on the R107 is often caused by the trunk seal deteriorating and taillamp housing gaskets failing — fix the water entry source before repairing the metal.

queries.query: The carpet is damp and I found rust holes in the floor when I pulled it back — I can see the ground underneath.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the rusted floor pan sections. The R107 floor pans are structural elements. Cut out the corroded areas, fabricate or source repair panels, and MIG weld in place. Seal all seams with structural seam sealer. Coat with epoxy primer and undercoating. Also identify and fix the water entry source — typically the windshield seal, door seals, or cowl area drains. NOTE: Floor pan rust on the R107 compromises structural rigidity. Professional repair on a rotisserie is ideal for extensive corrosion. Inspect the adjacent outrigger supports and crossmembers while floors are exposed.

queries.query: The bottom edges of the doors are bubbling with rust and the paint is peeling away.
queries.validated_fix: Repair or replace the door skins/lower sections. Sand, treat with rust converter, fill minor pitting, and repaint. For severe corrosion, weld in patch panels. Also check and replace the inner door moisture barriers — torn barriers allow water to sit against the door skin. Clear the door drain holes at the bottom edges. NOTE: Window frame rust is often caused by deteriorated window channel rubber — water runs down the glass and pools in the frame. Replace the window channel seals when repairing the frame.

queries.query: There's rust appearing around the edges of the windshield and I can see bubbling under the chrome trim.
queries.validated_fix: The R107 windshield is bonded to the body with adhesive — water intrusion between the glass and the body causes hidden corrosion. Remove the windshield, cut away the old adhesive, treat the rust with converter and epoxy primer, and reinstall the windshield with new urethane adhesive. Replace the windshield seal/molding. NOTE: The windshield bond is part of the R107's structural rigidity (important for a roadster without a fixed roof). Professional windshield removal and reinstallation is recommended to maintain structural integrity.

queries.query: The heater blows hot air constantly no matter what I set the controls to — I can't get air conditioning or even turn the heat off.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the climate control servo. The 1976-1980 R107 uses a vacuum/electric servo unit (mounted on the right side of the firewall under the hood) that controls the blend door position. The original plastic-cased servos crack and fail routinely. Replace with an aluminum-body replacement servo (aftermarket upgrade) or the Hamilton Motor Company electronic upgrade kit (~$700, replaces both the servo and amplifier with a digital unit). Also replace the amplifier behind the glove box if the original servo has failed. NOTE: Before replacing the servo, verify that the vacuum supply to the climate system is intact — a cracked vacuum hose to the servo can cause identical symptoms. The servo defaults to full heat when vacuum or electrical control is lost.

queries.query: Various things have stopped working — the door locks, the climate system vents, and the trunk lock actuator all seem affected.
queries.validated_fix: Replace all rubber vacuum hoses throughout the car. The R107 uses an extensive vacuum system to operate the climate control vent doors, central door locks, trunk lock, and some emission controls. After 50 years, the rubber hoses become brittle, crack, and leak. Replace all vacuum lines with new silicone or rubber vacuum tubing. Check the vacuum check valve(s) and the vacuum reservoir canister for leaks. Also check the engine vacuum source — low engine vacuum (from a worn engine or vacuum leak at the intake) starves all vacuum-operated systems. NOTE: A systematic replacement of ALL vacuum hoses during a restoration is the most efficient approach. Chasing individual leaks is time-consuming and new leaks will continue to appear as adjacent old hoses fail.

queries.query: The fan barely blows any air — even on the highest setting, I can barely feel airflow from the vents.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the blower motor (located in the heater box under the dashboard). Test by applying 12V directly — if it doesn't run or runs slowly, the motor is worn. Also check the blower motor resistor (for speed control) and the blower motor relay. On the R107, access to the blower motor requires significant dash disassembly. Also check the heater box front panel for cracks — a cracked housing allows air to bypass the blower. NOTE: While the heater box is open, also inspect the blend door and temperature flaps for proper operation — vacuum actuators on these flaps can also fail.

queries.query: The air conditioning doesn't blow cold air — the compressor either doesn't engage or barely cools.
queries.validated_fix: Diagnose the A/C system: (1) Check if the compressor clutch engages when A/C is selected — if not, check the A/C relay, pressure switch, and electrical circuit. (2) Check refrigerant level — the original R-12 system may have been converted to R-134a. Low charge = no cooling. (3) Check the compressor for mechanical failure (seized or noisy). (4) Check the condenser (in front of the radiator) for blockage or damage. (5) Check the receiver-drier for moisture contamination. A complete system evaluation with manifold gauges by an A/C specialist is recommended. NOTE: If the system still uses R-12, conversion to R-134a is recommended (requires new O-rings, receiver-drier, and sometimes a new compressor). Some owners retrofit with modern R-134a-compatible components for improved cooling.

queries.query: The dashboard has major cracks running across the top — the sun has destroyed the vinyl covering.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the dashboard pad. Reproduction R107 dashboard pads are available from specialists (Dashboard Designs, Recovery Room). The R107 dash pad is held in place with screws and clips — carefully remove the old pad, clean the metal frame, and install the new pad. Options: full replacement pad (original vinyl color/texture), dash cap (covers existing pad — less expensive but not original appearance), or professional crack repair (filler + texture spray for minor cracks). NOTE: R107 dash pads universally crack due to UV exposure and age — this is the most common interior complaint. A quality reproduction pad restores the interior dramatically.

queries.query: The driver's seat is cracked, torn, and the bolsters are collapsed — the material has given up after decades of use.
queries.technical_issue: Seat Upholstery Deterioration — MB-Tex or Leather
queries.validated_fix: Recover the seats with reproduction seat covers (MB-Tex vinyl or leather, available from Coco Mat, OEM Classic, or German specialists). Remove the seats from the car, strip the old covers, replace the foam (reproduction foam sets available), and install new covers using hog rings on the original seat frames. The R107 uses either MB-Tex (vinyl) or leather depending on the original specification. NOTE: Also rebuild the seat back recline mechanism while the seats are apart — the mechanism wears and causes the seat back to wobble. Replacement covers are available in original colors and materials.

queries.query: The driver's seat back is loose and wobbles back and forth — it doesn't lock in any position anymore.
queries.validated_fix: Rebuild or replace the seat back recline mechanism. The R107 uses a gear-type recliner that wears over time. Rebuild kits are available that include new gears and bearings. Remove the seat, disassemble the recliner housing, replace the worn gears, reassemble with proper lubrication. If the housing is cracked or severely worn, replace the entire mechanism. NOTE: This is a common problem on high-mileage R107s. The driver's seat is always the worst because of the weight transfer during acceleration and braking.

queries.query: The driver's window won't go up or down — the switch makes a sound but the glass doesn't move, or it's very slow.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the window regulator. The R107 uses electric window regulators (motor-driven gear mechanism). Common failures: stripped nylon gears inside the regulator, worn motor brushes, or broken lift cables. Remove the door panel, disconnect the old regulator from the glass, and install a new regulator assembly. Test the motor separately by applying 12V — if it runs but the window doesn't move, the internal gears are stripped. Replacement regulators are available from URO Parts, Lemforder, or Mercedes OE. NOTE: Also check the window switch (in the center console) — corroded contacts in the switch can prevent current from reaching the motor.

queries.query: The carpet is rotted and the car smells musty — years of moisture have ruined the interior flooring.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the carpet with a molded carpet set (available from Coco Mat, DashDesigns, or Mercedes specialists in original colors). Remove seats, console, and trim. Clean and treat the floor pans (address any rust before installing new carpet). Install new sound-deadening underlay and the molded carpet. NOTE: As with any classic car, fix all water leaks FIRST (windshield seal, door seals, cowl drains, heater core) before installing new carpet. Dampness returns quickly if the source isn't addressed.

queries.query: The convertible top leaks when it rains and the fabric is cracked, faded, and stiff.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the convertible top. R107 tops are available in original vinyl or optional canvas in factory colors (Electron Top, AutoTopsDirect, or German OE suppliers). Remove the old top, inspect the folding mechanism for proper operation, replace any worn bushings or latches, and install the new top. Replace the rear window (plastic or glass upgrade). Replace all top weatherstripping. NOTE: Professional installation is strongly recommended — R107 top installation requires precise tensioning for wrinkle-free fit and proper drainage. The new top must seal properly at the header (front) and at the rear window frame.

queries.query: Even with the hardtop on, water leaks in around the edges — the seals are dried out and don't seal anymore.
queries.technical_issue: Hardtop Seal Deterioration — Water Leaks with Top On
queries.validated_fix: Replace all hardtop seals — the header (front) seal, the side channel seals, and the rear seal. R107 hardtop seal kits are available from Metro Moulded, Steele Rubber, or Mercedes OE. Clean the mating surfaces on both the hardtop and the body before installing new seals. Verify proper alignment of the hardtop — the locating pins and front latches must engage correctly for the seals to compress properly. NOTE: The R107 hardtop is very heavy (~45 kg / 100 lbs) and requires two people or a top removal stand. Check the hardtop attachment latches and hinges for wear — loose hardware prevents proper seal compression.

queries.query: There's a ticking/hissing noise from the engine bay that varies with RPM — it sounds like an exhaust leak.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the exhaust manifold gaskets (both sides on the V8). The M117 uses cast-iron exhaust manifolds that can crack at the collector. If manifolds are cracked, replace them. Check the manifold studs/bolts — they commonly break due to heat cycling. Extract broken studs with an EZ-Out or drill-and-retap. Apply anti-seize to new bolts. NOTE: On the 1976 US-spec car, the catalytic converters are mounted directly on the exhaust manifolds ('warm-up catalysts'). Check these for damage, clogging, and the associated heat shielding. The extreme heat from these under-hood cats damages nearby wiring, hoses, and components.

queries.query: The engine lacks power and runs hot — I think the catalytic converters may be plugged up.
queries.validated_fix: Inspect the manifold-mounted catalytic converters. The 1975-1976 US-spec 450SL has 'warm-up' catalysts bolted directly to the exhaust manifolds. These can clog over time (especially if the engine has been running rich), causing back-pressure that reduces power and increases operating temperature. Test: check exhaust back-pressure at the oxygen sensor port or manifold — excessive pressure indicates a clogged cat. Replace with new catalytic converters if clogged. Some owners relocate the converters downstream (where later R107 models had them) to reduce underhood heat — verify emissions legality in your area before doing this. NOTE: The underhood cat placement is widely regarded as the worst design feature of the 1975-1976 450SL. The heat they generate causes premature failure of wiring, hoses, starter motors, and fuel system components. Any reduction in underhood heat exposure significantly improves reliability.

queries.query: The engine runs rough at idle and there's a hesitation during acceleration — also occasionally stalls.
queries.validated_fix: Clean or replace the EGR (Exhaust Gas Recirculation) valve. On the 1976 450SL, the EGR valve is mounted on the left (driver's side) exhaust manifold. Carbon buildup from exhaust gases causes the valve to stick open (rough idle/stalling) or stick closed (increased NOx emissions). Remove the valve, clean thoroughly with carburetor cleaner and a wire brush, and test for proper vacuum-actuated operation. Replace if the diaphragm is damaged or the valve is excessively worn. Also check the EGR vacuum switching valve and its vacuum supply. NOTE: A stuck-open EGR valve at idle introduces exhaust gas into the intake, diluting the fresh charge and causing a rough/unstable idle. This is a common and often overlooked cause of drivability problems.

queries.query: There's a vibration from underneath the car and a clunking sound when shifting from Park to Drive.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the driveshaft flex disc(s) (Guibo). The R107 has a flex disc at the front of the driveshaft (at the transmission output) and possibly one at the rear (at the differential input). The rubber disc develops cracks and tears over time, causing vibration and clunking. Replace with a Febi or Lemforder flex disc. Also check the center support bearing on the two-piece driveshaft — a failed bearing causes vibration and noise. NOTE: Always check the driveshaft U-joints as well — worn U-joints cause similar symptoms. Replace everything together as a drivetrain service.

queries.query: There's gear oil dripping from the rear axle area — I can see it on the inside of the rear wheels.
queries.technical_issue: Differential Seal Leak / Rear Axle Seal Leak
queries.validated_fix: Replace the differential pinion seal (at the front of the differential) and/or the rear axle shaft seals. The R107's independent rear suspension uses CV joints (constant velocity joints) at the axle shafts — the rubber CV boots can tear and leak grease (separate from the differential oil seals). Inspect and replace CV boots if torn. Replace differential seals with Corteco or Elring units. Refill with the correct gear oil (SAE 90 GL-5, or limited-slip additive if applicable). NOTE: If the differential itself is whining or howling, the ring and pinion may need setup adjustment or replacement — this is specialist work.

queries.query: The car is very loud inside from wind noise around the doors and water gets in when it rains.
queries.technical_issue: Door Seal Deterioration — Wind Noise / Water Leak
queries.validated_fix: Replace all door weatherstripping. Complete door seal kits are available for the R107 from Mercedes OE, Metro Moulded, or Steele Rubber. Replace the door frame seal (on the door), the body-side seal (on the opening), and the window run channels. Clean all old adhesive residue before applying new seals with 3M weatherstrip adhesive. Verify proper door alignment and latch engagement before blaming the seals — a misaligned door won't seal regardless of seal condition. NOTE: Also replace the windshield and rear window seals if original — they also harden and leak over 50 years.

queries.query: Water gets into the trunk every time it rains — the seal around the trunk lid is dried out and hard.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the trunk lid seal with a new gasket (Mercedes OE or Steele Rubber). Clean the mating surfaces on both the lid and the body. Also check and replace the taillamp housing gaskets — water enters around the taillamp bezels and runs into the trunk channel. Clear the trunk gutter drain holes. NOTE: Verify trunk lid alignment — the hinge springs weaken over time and can prevent the lid from seating evenly against the new seal.

queries.query: The engine runs rough and seems to hunt between rich and lean — the exhaust smells like gas sometimes and the idle surges.
queries.technical_issue: Fuel System Vacuum Leak — Rich/Lean Running
queries.validated_fix: The Bosch CIS system is extremely sensitive to vacuum leaks. Systematically check all vacuum hoses, the brake booster connection, the CIS warm-up regulator vacuum line, the EGR valve, the PCV/crankcase vent system, and the intake manifold gaskets. Use propane enrichment or carburetor cleaner spray to locate leaks (RPM change = leak found). Replace all cracked or deteriorated vacuum hoses. NOTE: A single vacuum leak after the air sensor plate causes unmetered air to enter the engine, which the CIS system cannot compensate for. This results in a lean condition that no CIS adjustment can fix. Finding and fixing every vacuum leak is the #1 most important maintenance step for CIS driveability.

queries.query: Oil is being pushed out of every gasket and there's oily residue around the oil filler cap and breather.
queries.validated_fix: Check and restore the crankcase ventilation system. The M117 uses a PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) system that routes blowby gases from the crankcase back to the intake. Check the PCV valve for proper function (it should rattle when shaken). Replace the PCV valve and all crankcase vent hoses. If the system is clean and functional but pressure persists, excessive piston ring blowby is the cause — a compression and leakdown test will confirm. NOTE: A clogged PCV system pressurizes the crankcase, which forces oil past every gasket and seal. Restoring proper crankcase ventilation often dramatically reduces oil leaks without replacing gaskets.

queries.query: The radio antenna won't go up or down — it's stuck in one position and I can hear the motor straining.
queries.technical_issue: Antenna Mast — Stuck or Broken Power Antenna
queries.validated_fix: Replace the antenna mast (the toothed nylon cord inside the mast tube strips over time). Replacement mast kits include a new mast and nylon drive cord. Remove the old mast, feed the new cord into the motor, and install the new mast. If the motor itself has failed, replace the entire antenna assembly. NOTE: The R107 uses a Hirschmann power antenna. Clean the mast tube and apply silicone spray to keep the mast moving freely. A stuck mast can burn out the motor if left untreated.

queries.query: The wood trim on the dashboard and console is peeling, cracking, and delaminating from the backing.
queries.technical_issue: Zebra Wood / Burl Wood Trim Delamination
queries.validated_fix: Restore or replace the wood trim. Options: (1) Professional restoration — strip the old finish, repair/re-veneer if needed, and apply multiple coats of UV-resistant clear lacquer. Several specialists offer R107 wood trim restoration by mail. (2) Replace with new reproduction trim panels (available from Mercedes trim specialists). (3) DIY refinish — carefully strip old finish with chemical stripper, sand lightly, repair delamination with wood glue and clamps, and refinish with automotive-grade clear coat. NOTE: The R107 wood trim is real wood veneer over a substrate — exposure to UV light and temperature extremes causes the finish and veneer to fail. Restored trim dramatically improves the interior appearance.

queries.query: The rear of the car sags and doesn't self-level anymore — it used to maintain ride height regardless of load.
queries.validated_fix: The optional self-leveling rear suspension uses hydraulic assist shocks connected to a hydraulic pump. If equipped and failed: (1) Replace the self-leveling shock absorbers (expensive). (2) Convert to standard non-self-leveling shocks (more common and cost-effective — Bilstein or KYB). The conversion requires replacing the rear shocks with standard units and removing/bypassing the hydraulic pump and lines. NOTE: Many R107 owners opt for the conversion to standard shocks because the self-leveling components are expensive and failure-prone. Standard high-quality shocks provide excellent ride quality.

queries.query: The parking brake doesn't hold the car on a hill — the handle pulls up but the car still rolls.
queries.technical_issue: Parking Brake Cable Seized / Not Holding
queries.validated_fix: Replace the parking brake cables (front cable and both rear cables). The cables corrode inside their sheaths and seize. New cables are available from ATE, Gemo, or Mercedes OE. Adjust the rear brake shoes/pads and then adjust the cable at the equalizer until the brake holds firmly at 3-5 clicks. Also inspect the parking brake shoe/pad mechanism at the rear wheels — the actuation levers can seize from corrosion. NOTE: On R107s with rear drum brakes, the parking brake uses the rear brake shoes. On disc-brake rear ends, there's a small drum-in-hat mechanism inside the rear rotor.

queries.query: The oil pressure gauge reads very low or zero even though the engine sounds fine and isn't knocking.
queries.validated_fix: Verify actual oil pressure with a mechanical gauge before assuming the worst. Remove the oil pressure sender from the engine and temporarily install a mechanical gauge. The M117 should read 3-4 bar at 2,000 RPM when warm, and at least 0.5-0.8 bar at hot idle. If mechanical pressure is normal, replace the oil pressure sender (VDO or Mercedes OE) and check the dash gauge and wiring. If pressure is genuinely low: check oil level and condition, replace the oil filter, and consider an oil pump replacement or engine bearing inspection. NOTE: The M117's oil pressure sender is a known failure item — it often gives false low readings. Always verify with a mechanical gauge before panicking.

queries.query: Multiple electrical systems died at once — no headlights, no starter, no accessories. The car is completely dead.
queries.technical_issue: Fusible Link / Main Power Feed Failure
queries.validated_fix: Check the fusible links. The R107 has fusible links (small-gauge sacrificial wire sections) in the main power distribution circuit near the battery and starter. If a fusible link has burned through, it can cut power to large sections of the electrical system. The burned section may look intact externally (the insulation hides the break inside). Test by checking for voltage on both sides of the link. Replace with the correct gauge fusible link wire — never use regular wire or a higher-gauge link. Also check the main battery cable connections and the starter solenoid terminals. NOTE: A fusible link typically burns because of a short circuit somewhere in the system — diagnose and fix the short before installing the new link.

queries.query: The engine has lost power gradually and one bank seems weaker than the other — uneven exhaust note.
queries.validated_fix: Inspect camshaft lobes for wear. Remove valve covers and visually check each lobe — worn lobes appear flat or concave on the nose. Measure with a micrometer and compare to spec. If worn, replace the camshaft and affected cam followers/rocker arms. Install with ZDDP-rich assembly lube. Break in at 2,000 RPM for 20 minutes. Use oil with adequate ZDDP (zinc) content — Valvoline VR1, Brad Penn, or add ZDDPlus supplement. NOTE: Modern low-ZDDP oils accelerate flat-tappet cam wear on the M117. Always use ZDDP-rich oil in this engine.

queries.query: Coolant is leaking from the side of the engine block from a corroded round metal plug.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the leaking freeze plug(s). Clean the bore, install a new brass plug (corrosion-resistant) using a proper driver tool. If the bore is damaged, an expandable rubber plug (Dorman) can be used. NOTE: If multiple plugs leak, flush the entire cooling system with chemical cleaner, rinse thoroughly, and refill with proper 50/50 coolant. Replace ALL freeze plugs proactively when the engine is removed for other service.

queries.query: The car takes a very long time to start after sitting for even just a couple of hours.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the check valve in the fuel pump or the fuel pump itself. The check valve prevents fuel from draining back to the tank after shutdown. Test: pinch the fuel supply line immediately after shutdown — if the next start is improved, the check valve is leaking. Replace the fuel pump (Bosch 0580254957 or equivalent) — the check valve is internal and not separately serviceable. NOTE: Also check the fuel accumulator — both the check valve and accumulator work together to maintain residual pressure.

queries.query: The climate control system stopped working and I traced it to a failed water pump under the hood.
queries.technical_issue: Auxiliary Water Pump Failure (Climate Control Circuit)
queries.validated_fix: Replace the auxiliary water pump. The R107 has a small electric pump that circulates coolant through the heater core independently of engine RPM. When this pump seizes, it draws excessive current through the climate control panel's circuit board, burning it out. Replace the pump (firewall, passenger side) and inspect the climate panel for burned traces. Replace if damaged. NOTE: Known design flaw — add an inline fuse to the pump circuit to protect the expensive climate panel from future pump failures.

queries.query: My turn signals don't flash — they either stay on solid or don't illuminate at all.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the combined turn signal / hazard relay (in the relay panel above the fuse box). The relay provides the flashing function. Replace with a genuine Mercedes or SWF relay. Also check the hazard switch on the steering column — corroded contacts prevent operation. NOTE: A burned-out bulb on one side can prevent flashing (no load on the circuit) — always check all bulbs before replacing the relay.

queries.query: The steering wheel shakes violently at certain speeds, especially after hitting a bump.
queries.technical_issue: Steering Damper Wear — Shimmy at Speed
queries.validated_fix: Replace the steering damper (Stabilus 0004635132 or Bilstein equivalent). The steering damper is a hydraulic shock absorber between the steering linkage and frame. When worn, the steering is prone to shimmy triggered by road imperfections. Also check and tighten the steering box mounting bolts and replace any worn steering linkage components. NOTE: A new steering damper dramatically improves R107 steering feel. It's an inexpensive, easy-to-replace part that is often overlooked.

queries.query: The front fenders have rust developing along the bottom edges and behind the chrome trim strips.
queries.technical_issue: Front Fender Rust — Lower Edge and Behind Trim
queries.validated_fix: Repair or replace rusted fender sections. Sand, treat with rust converter, weld patch panels if needed, repaint. Check behind all body trim strips — moisture trapped behind trim accelerates corrosion. After repair, ensure fender drainage holes are clear and apply cavity wax. NOTE: R107 front fenders are bolt-on (not welded) — replacement with new or NOS fenders is relatively straightforward.

queries.query: The sun visors are sagging, the vinyl is cracked, and they won't stay in the up position anymore.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the sun visors with reproduction units (available in original colors). The R107 visors clip onto mounting brackets with a spring mechanism. Replace retaining clips if broken. If the vanity mirror is cracked or de-silvered, reproduction mirrors are available. NOTE: Visors are one of the first interior components to deteriorate from UV exposure — quality reproductions restore the interior significantly.

queries.query: A radiator hose burst while driving and the engine overheated immediately — coolant sprayed everywhere.
queries.validated_fix: Replace ALL coolant hoses — upper, lower, heater, and bypass. On a 50-year-old car, all rubber hoses are suspect even if they look okay externally — they deteriorate from the inside. Use Gates, Continental, or Mercedes hoses. Replace all clamps with proper German-style hose clamps (Norma or ABA). NOTE: Carry a spare upper hose and a gallon of coolant in the trunk as emergency supplies.

queries.query: The transmission won't downshift when I floor the gas pedal — I have no passing power on the highway.
queries.validated_fix: Adjust the kickdown cable (Bowden cable) from the throttle to the transmission. The cable must have proper free play and full travel. Also check the kickdown switch at the throttle pedal (under carpet). Clean or replace the switch if corroded. Adjust cable per factory specification. NOTE: Proper kickdown cable adjustment also affects all shift points — not just wide-open-throttle downshifts. Improper adjustment causes generally poor shift behavior.

queries.query: The front end clunks and the steering feels vague — there's movement in the subframe when I brake.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the front subframe mount bushings. The R107 subframe is mounted to the body via large rubber bushings that deteriorate over time. Use Lemforder or Meyle replacements (~$400+ for the set). This requires supporting the engine/subframe and partially separating the subframe from the body. NOTE: Labor-intensive but the improvement in handling and steering precision is transformative. Often combined with the subframe recall repair and a complete front-end rebuild.

queries.query: The battery tray in the trunk is corroded from acid — the metal is eaten away and the battery isn't secure.
queries.validated_fix: Clean and repair the battery tray. Remove battery, neutralize acid with baking soda and water, wire brush all corrosion, treat with rust converter, coat with acid-resistant paint (POR-15). If perforated, weld in new metal. Install new hold-down bracket. Coat terminals with anti-corrosion spray. NOTE: The R107 has the battery in the trunk (right side). Check surrounding trunk floor — acid migration corrodes adjacent areas. Keep the battery vent tube connected and routed outside.

queries.query: The air pump makes a loud squealing or grinding noise from the engine bay.
queries.validated_fix: Replace or rebuild the air injection (AIR) pump. The 1976 US-spec 450SL has a belt-driven pump that injects fresh air into the exhaust manifolds for catalytic converter efficiency. If pump bearings fail, it produces grinding/squealing noise. Also check the air pump check valves and diverter valve. NOTE: Required for emissions compliance in inspection states. In non-emission states, some owners remove it — verify legality first. A non-functional air pump does not affect driveability.

queries.query: The shift pattern on the console is worn off and the shift gate plastic is cracked or broken.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the shift gate bezel and indicator. Reproduction bezels and shift pattern overlays are available for the R107. Remove console trim, replace bezel and overlay, reassemble. Also replace shift knob if worn. NOTE: While the console is apart, inspect window switches and climate control panel and their electrical connections.

queries.query: I hear a clicking noise from the rear wheels when turning and there's grease splattered on the inside of the wheel.
queries.technical_issue: CV Joint Boot Tear / CV Joint Failure
queries.validated_fix: Replace the torn CV boot(s) and repack with fresh CV grease, or replace the entire CV joint if it has been running without grease and is now worn (clicking sound = worn). The R107 independent rear suspension uses CV joints (constant velocity joints) at the rear axle half-shafts. A torn boot allows grease to escape and contamination to enter, destroying the joint. If caught early (boot torn but no clicking), replacing the boot and repacking is sufficient. If clicking, the joint must be replaced. NOTE: Also check the inner CV joints/flex discs at the differential output flanges.

queries.query: Water drips down the inside of the windshield when it rains — it runs down the A-pillar and onto the dash.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the windshield seal. The R107 windshield is bonded with urethane adhesive. Remove the windshield (requires piano wire or specialized cutting tool and a helper), clean all old adhesive from the body pinchweld and the glass edge, treat any rust with converter and primer, and reinstall with new urethane adhesive (3M or Sika). Replace the chrome or rubber windshield moldings. NOTE: The windshield bond is structural on the R107 — it provides body rigidity critical for an open-top car. Professional installation is strongly recommended. Also inspect the cowl/scuttle area for rust while the windshield is out — this is an excellent time to address hidden corrosion.

queries.query: The brake pedal is very hard to push and the car barely stops — it takes all my strength.
queries.validated_fix: Replace the brake vacuum booster. Test: with the engine off, pump the brake pedal several times to deplete vacuum, then hold the pedal and start the engine — the pedal should drop noticeably as vacuum assist engages. If it doesn't, the booster has failed. Also check the vacuum check valve and the vacuum supply hose from the intake manifold. Replace the booster with a rebuilt or new unit (ATE or FTE). Bench-bleed and install the master cylinder onto the new booster. Bleed the entire brake system. NOTE: The booster is behind the master cylinder on the firewall. Replacement requires disconnecting brake lines and the master cylinder — bleed everything after reassembly.

queries.query: A mechanic tried to jack up the car and the jack point collapsed — the metal underneath is rusted through.
queries.validated_fix: Repair/reinforce the jack points and outriggers. The R107's jack points are integrated into the sill/outrigger structure. If corroded, the damaged sections must be cut out and new metal welded in. Reinforcement plates can be welded over the repairs. Coat with epoxy primer and undercoating after repair. NOTE: Damaged jack points indicate that the sill structure may also be compromised — inspect the entire sill area when repairing jack points. Using the car's jack on a corroded point can cause the car to fall off the jack — always use proper jack stands on solid structural points.
